acceptance_criteria: expert_memo.md contains at least one exact formula string and one numeric sensitivity worked example.
alarm_fatigue_4.xlsx contains a Formula_Audit sheet with formula text for the listed cells OR an attached screenshot/CSV with identical formula strings.
agent_trace.json documents creation of the audit artifacts.
Metadata and grader/verifier outputs are internally consistent about whether semantic checks were performed.
files_to_update: attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] -> alarm_fatigue_4.xlsx (add Formula_Audit sheet or attach formulas file)
attempts/baseline/agent_trace.json -> agent_trace.json (add step describing audit artifacts)
tests_to_run_after_revision: Open alarm_fatigue_4.xlsx in a full environment and verify formula text in the stated ranges matches Formula_Audit entries or screenshot.
Confirm numeric sensitivity example in memo reproduces AB column statuses when perturbations are applied.
Ensure metadata no longer claims 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated' while including semantic scores.

planned_changes: Update expert_memo.md to include the exact formula text for a representative derived metric cell (copy-paste the formula string) and add a brief numeric sensitivity worked example showing perturbed inputs and resulting classification.

Modify alarm_fatigue_4.xlsx to add a new sheet 'Formula_Audit' that lists (cell reference -> formula text) for ranges I2:I4, M2:M4, Q2:Q4, U2:U4, AB2:AB4. If adding the sheet is impractical, include a screenshot PNG and a CSV/plaintext file containing those formula strings.
Amend agent_trace.json to include an explicit step noting creation of the Formula_Audit sheet (or screenshot/CSV) and point reviewers to those artifacts.

Correct grader/attempt metadata to avoid contradictory flags about semantic evaluation: either run reproducible semantic checks or remove semantic score fields and explain reliance on memo+trace evidence.
Re-run the verification step and attach any additional artifacts (screenshot/CSV) so the verifier can confirm formulas without openpyxl.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Remove residual uncertainty caused by inability to parse the .xlsx in the verifier environment and fix an internal metadata inconsistency about semantic evaluation. Make formulas and sensitivity checks explicitly visible and verifiable without requiring openpyxl.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline meets the artifact contract and the grader/verifier passed the attempt. Two residual concerns: (1) the evaluation environment could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable), so exact cell-by-cell formula verification was not performed; (2) grader metadata shows an internal inconsistency about whether semantic correctness was evaluated (some fields state 'not evaluated' while semantic scores are present). Provide clearer, verifiable artifacts to remove residual uncertainty.
